title: Nostolachma jenkinsii
taxonomy: Nostolachma jenkinsii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Nostolachma, which is a part of the tribe Naucleateae. The plant is native to the Himalayan region and is known for its unique characteristics. Further classification of Nostolachma jenkinsii includes its order (Asterales) and class (Magnoliopsida). The classification of Nostolachma jenkinsii is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.
uses: Nostolachma jenkinsii is reported to have various medicinal properties due to its bioactive compounds. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions. Its extracts have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in treating infections and reducing inflammation. Additionally, it is also used to treat fever and cough. Further research is required to fully explore its potential as a medicinal plant.
